content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to celebrate Christmas by travelling around the world.
He plans to visit a certain number of countries, and in **each country**, he will visit exactly **3 locations**.

Given the number of countries Chef plans to visit, determine the **total number of locations** he will visit.

**Function Description**Function NametotalLocationstotalLocationstotalLocations

Input Parameters
NNN : number of countries.
Return Value
Integer representing the total number of locations Chef will visit.
**Constraints**
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤N≤1001 \leq N \leq 1001≤N≤100

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer
TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case contains a single integer
NNN — the number of countries Chef wants to visit.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer — the **total number of locations** Chef will visit.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of countries Chef will visit.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the total number of locations Chef will visit (3 × N).

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: 10 countries → 10 × 3 = 30 locations. Second test case: 1 country → 1 × 3 = 3 locations.
Although N is constrained to be at least 1, this sample shows the formula works for any non‑negative N: multiply by 3.

solution.approach: For each test case read N and compute 3 * N. The multiplication is O(1). Repeat for all T test cases.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
out = []
idx = 1
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(data[idx])
    idx += 1
    out.append(str(3 * N))
print('\n'.join(out))
